verdict.answer_first: A leads with the command path: "Use `find`..."; B opens with context and "a few good options" before the command.
verdict.decisiveness: A gives one primary prescription first; B explicitly surveys: "Here are a few good options" plus extra Homebrew/coreutils/depth variants.
verdict.fabrication: B likely overclaims macOS support with "Add ... `-maxdepth 1`"; BSD/macOS `find` commonly lacks GNU `-maxdepth`. A: none found.
verdict.actionability: A is easier to execute: one correct macOS command, byte-size meaning, and only two practical caveats; B adds more branches and one questionable tip.
